_meta.purpose: Retroactive engagement-review scores for stories shipped before the stage-3.5 gate existed. Establishes a baseline for future authoring and a triage list for revisits.
_meta.rubric: hook | voice | originality | coherence | closure (1-5 each)
history: 2026-04-22 — stories 2 and 3 revised after baseline put them under the bar (2.8 and 3.4)

2026-04-22 — story 5 (朝の散歩) authored end-to-end through the tightened pipeline (planner → writer with engagement guidance → validate → engagement-review → ship); cleared the bar on first pass (avg 4.6)

2026-04-22 — story 6 (猫) authored end-to-end; first story with a non-human character (cat as new presence). Cleared the bar on first pass (avg 4.6, ties story 5 for #1). G002_ga_subject (in state since story 1 but barely used) gets two real textual uses presenting the cat as new information.

2026-04-22 — JP NLP toolkit installed (fugashi + jaconv + jamdict-data) and wired into precheck/scaffold/lookup. Real inflection-engine validation, JMdict auto-fill of new word definitions, English↔JP dictionary lookup CLI, morphological-analysis CLI.

2026-04-22 — story 7 (夜) authored end-to-end with the new tooling (scaffold --new-word-surfaces auto-filled 夜/月/星 from JMdict; precheck --fix auto-computed all_words_used). First night-set story; gives G011_ya_partial real semantic work (was in state since story 4, only used twice). Cleared the bar on first pass (avg 4.4).

2026-04-22 — story 8 (本と友達) authored end-to-end. First multi-person scene with parallel actions in the library. Gives G009_mo_also (formerly 4 spread-thin uses) 3 fresh load-bearing semantic uses in one tight a/b/a/b/c construction. Cleared the bar on first pass (avg 4.6, ties stories 5 and 6 for #1).

2026-04-22 — story 9 (朝の猫) authored end-to-end. First story to use a recurring character (cat from story 6) — a real arrival of multi-story narrative continuity. Gives G004_ni_location (was the lowest-used grammar at 1 use across 8 stories) real semantic work — every sentence answers 'where?'. Sleeping-cat compound-noun closer is a new syntactic pattern. Cleared the bar on first pass (avg 4.4).

2026-04-22 — story 10 (友達からの手紙) authored end-to-end. First story to exercise the new fugashi-classified irregular_kuru verb path (来ます). Gives G006_kara_from real semantic work — letter FROM the friend, friend FROM far away. First off-stage character communication: friend exists only via the letter, not as a present body. Closer breaks the 'X, feeling です' pattern (used in 5 of 9 prior stories) — ends on the verb 待ちます (open-ended waiting). Cleared the bar on first pass (avg 4.6, ties stories 5/6/8 for #1). Validates with zero warnings — first ship after the v0.8 inflection-engine consolidation.

highlights: Opening line 「今朝は雨です。」 lands the reader in a specific time + weather in 7 syllables.
Sentence 2 「木が濡れています。」 is the only image line — quiet, concrete, earned.
Closing 「今朝はいい気分です」 returns to the time-stamped opener; symmetry feels intentional, not lazy.
weaknesses: Sentences 3–5 alternate 私は + state descriptions; reads slightly listy.
Originality is constrained — this is the bootstrap story, 14 new words. Voice still emerges.

suggestions.what: Consider swapping s3 ('I drink tea') and s4 ('the rain is quiet') to delay the I-action and let the world breathe first.
If a future revision pass adds it, replace 'いい気分です' with something more specific like '心が静かです' once 心 enters vocabulary.

highlights: Hook now mirrors story 4: time-of-day comma, companion, action in one breath.
Sentence 2 「雨は静かです。」 personifies the rain — a small voice move that opens the scene.
Two も uses (s3, s5) carry an actual reciprocity between narrator and friend, not just particle decoration.
Closing 「雨と夕方と公園を歩きます」 returns to the verb of s0 with three new motifs as object — circular and earned.
weaknesses: Implied scene shift between walking (s1) and tea (s3); a single linking sentence would smooth it. Deferred to a future story (needs verbs like 座る, 休む).

suggestions.what: If a future revision pass adds 座ります or 休みます, insert a one-line 'we sit' beat between s2 and s3.

revision_notes: Removed 4 of the 5 静か uses; kept exactly one in s2.
Replaced 'warm at evening' with 'tea is warm' — temperature claim now anchored to a thing, not the air.
Rewrote the closer from 「気分もいいです」 (orphaned feeling) to 「雨と夕方と公園を歩きます」 (motif-list-as-object, returns to verb of opener).

highlights: Hook 「朝、雨です。」 is a 4-syllable sensory poem — specific weather + time in two beats.
Closing 「雨の外と静かな朝、いい気分です」 pulls weather + time + feeling into a single line — strongest closer in the set.
Subject-drop in s3, s5, s6 lets the narrator feel less like a worksheet 私.
weaknesses: Still essentially a 'breakfast scene' — originality remains the hardest dimension to push within the constraint.

suggestions.what: If a future revision pass introduces a sound or tactile word (e.g. 音 / 香り), replace one of the 温かい lines with the new sense.

revision_notes: Replaced flat opener 「朝ごはんは卵とお茶です」 (definition) with 「朝、雨です」 (sensory).
Split breakfast definition into a real beat (s2) — now arrives after the sensory frame is set.
Removed redundant s5 (was duplicate of s1 with English 'after that' that didn't exist in JP); replaced with a separate 食べます beat that actually advances the action.
Rewrote the closer to merge weather + time + feeling into one image.

highlights: Opening 「夕方、私は友達と公園を歩きます。」 is the strongest hook in the set: time-of-day comma, action verb, companion in one breath.
Sentence 5 「友達も飲みます。」 is the most specific human gesture in the four stories — a real, small, observed reciprocity.
Closing 「散歩と友達はいい気分です」 lifts the two motifs into a single line of feeling that feels earned, not decorated.
weaknesses: Mid-story scene transition is implied: the friend and narrator are walking, then suddenly drinking tea (s4). One linking sentence (or a different verb than 歩きます after the tea) would help.
「いい気分」 appears in s3 AND s6. The closer would land harder if s3's feeling were swapped for a sensory observation.
[Closer rewritten 2026-04-22 (v0.14) to break the いい〜です template echo across stories 1-9.]

suggestions.what: Insert a one-line 'we sit' or 'we rest' beat between s3 and s4 — needs a new word like 座ります, plan it for a future story.
Replace s3 「散歩はいい気分です」 with a sensory line so 'いい気分' lands only once, in the closer.

highlights: First story authored end-to-end with the engagement rubric in the writer prompt — cleared the bar on first pass (no revision cycle).
Hook 「朝、私は公園を歩きます。」 follows the proven time-comma + action pattern.
Sentence 5 「そして、風です。」 — one-word weather sentence after the door observation; the wind arrives almost as a character. Most surprising line in the library.
Two そして's deliver real chronological structure (no gloss inflation — they exist in the JP, not just the English).
Closing 「風と雨、いい朝です。」 ties the two new sensory motifs (風 from s5, 雨 from s4) back to the opening 朝.
weaknesses: Optional: 私は in the hook could be dropped for an even tighter opener — but the explicit subject also reads naturally.

suggestions.what: If 音 (sound) enters vocab in a future story, revisit s5 to add an auditory note about the wind.

highlights: First story in the library with a non-human character — the cat is a real arrival; every prior story had only the narrator (and once a friend).

G002_ga_subject (in state since story 1 but barely used) finally gets real textual work: が presents the cat as new information in s2 and s4, then は picks it up as the topic in s3 and s5 — the textbook contrast made concrete.
Two そして's continue the textual-chronology pattern story 5 established (no gloss inflation).
Sentence 5 「そして、猫は私を見ます。」 is the best moment — the reciprocal gaze gives the narrator a second presence to react to.
Closer 「猫と私、いい朝です。」 mirrors story 4/5's 'A and B, feeling' shape with felt content shift: the 'and' is across species.
weaknesses: Hook (4 not 5): time + weather without an action — story 5's 「朝、私は公園を歩きます」 sets a stronger sensory stage. Acceptable trade because the cat itself is the surprise.
[Closer rewritten 2026-04-22 (v0.14) to break the いい〜です template echo across stories 1-9.]

suggestions.what: When 中 (inside) enters vocab, swap s4 to 「猫は中にいます」 and free a sentence for a third specific gesture.
When 降ります (to fall, of rain) enters vocab, change s0 to 「雨が降ります」 to make the hook a sensory action rather than a stative.

highlights: First night-set story in the library — fills the obvious gap (5 morning + 1 evening before this).

Doubles down on the engagement playbook stories 5 and 6 established: そして for textual chronology (×2), が for new-information presentation, and the 'give underused grammar real work' move applied to G011_ya_partial.
や was in state since story 4 but used only twice; story 7 gives it two new semantic uses ('and other things'), establishing it as a real unit.
Two も's tie the moon and the night ('also...') into the narrator's stillness.
Sentence 4 「月も雨を見ます」 is the most original beat: where story 6's cat looked back at the narrator, the moon's gaze passes through the narrator entirely toward the rain — a different shape of attention.
Closer 「月と夜、いい気分です。」 ties heavenly body + time-of-day.
weaknesses: Hook (4 not 5): time + image (the moon) is stative — 月です reads slightly flat. Could become a sensory action when a verb of appearance enters vocab.
Originality (4 not 5): the imagery (moon/stars/rain/quiet) is conventional Japanese poetic stock. The grammar choreography (や/と/も interplay) is what carries the freshness.
[Closer rewritten 2026-04-22 (v0.14) to break the いい〜です template echo across stories 1-9.]

suggestions.what: When 遠く ('far away') enters vocab, replace s4's 雨 with 遠く: 「月も遠くを見ます」 lands the contrast with story 6's reciprocal cat more strongly.
When a verb of appearance (e.g. 出る 'to come out') enters vocab, change s0 to 「月が出ます」 to make the hook a sensory action rather than stative.

highlights: First multi-person scene with parallel actions in the library — every prior story had a single observer (with one cameo friend in story 4 as a reciprocal partner, not a parallel one).
Structure IS the originality: an a/b/a/b/c pattern where each も does real semantic work as a partner-equivalence marker.

G009_mo_also was at 4 spread-thin uses across 4 stories before this; story 8 adds 3 fresh uses all doing real comparison work in one tight construction (s2 'friend ALSO reads', s4 'friend's book ALSO is quiet', s5 'tea TOO is there').
Closer 「二人と本、いい朝です。」 collapses the parallel into 二人 as a unit — the word from the subtitle finally lands as the synthesis.
Notable absence: no そして's. The story doesn't need chronological connectors because every beat is in the same continuous moment — different from stories 5/7's chronological structure.
weaknesses: Hook (4 not 5): time + weather is the proven anchor but doesn't itself carry originality — the originality lives in the parallel structure that follows.
Voice (4 not 5): narrator-as-observer is consistent but the parallel symmetry is so even it could read slightly mechanical; a small asymmetry in the future would deepen it.
[Closer rewritten 2026-04-22 (v0.14) to break the いい〜です template echo across stories 1-9.]

suggestions.what: When 隣 ('next to') enters vocab, add a position-grounding line between s0 and s1 — '友達は私の隣にいます' — to anchor the spatial relationship before the parallel begins.
When 違う ('different') enters vocab, replace s4 with '友達の本は私の本と違います' to make the parallel slightly less perfectly symmetric, adding a small tension under the surface calm.

highlights: first story to use a recurring character (cat from story 6) — narrative continuity across stories
cat-narrator-cat attention triangle (s0 cat sits → s1 I look → s4 cat looks at book) is a structurally fresh move
に carries semantic load throughout — every sentence answers 'where?'
sleeping-cat compound-noun closer (寝る猫) is a new syntactic pattern in the library
weaknesses: hook is locational, not sensory (4 not 5)
'good morning' closer phrasing reused from stories 5/6/8 (4 not 5)
[Closer rewritten 2026-04-22 (v0.14) to break the いい〜です template echo across stories 1-9.]
next_actions: consider opening with a sensory cue (sound, smell, light) when story 10 targets a hook of 5
the 'X, feeling です' closer pattern is now used in 5 of 9 stories — vary further as the library grows
G006_kara_from is the next-most-underused grammar (2 uses) — candidate for a 'real work' move in story 10

suggestions.what: Consider weaving in a sensory detail in s2-s4 to give the friend's mention more presence; right now the friend is purely conceptual.

highlights: first true sensory hook (wind opens the scene before any human appears)
first off-stage character communication — friend exists only via the letter
first irregular verb shipped (来ます, irregular_kuru) — exercises new fugashi pipeline path
closer breaks the 'X, feeling です' pattern with an open-ended verb (待ちます)
から carries semantic load throughout (FROM friend, FROM far away)
weaknesses: s5 ('friend drinks tea too') is a thematically sideways turn that doesn't directly serve the wait/letter arc — slight coherence ding (4 not 5)
voice's 'wait for friend's morning' phrasing is gloss-accurate but slightly unusual
next_actions: G007_te_form is now the most underused grammar (2 uses) — candidate for story 11's 'real work' move
if revising for 5/5 coherence: cut s5 (friend's tea) and let the two waits face each other directly
consider authoring story 11 with a single sustained action (te-form chaining)

suggestions.what: The waiting motif could be extended in a story-12 sequel: what arrives, what doesn't. 待ちます currently has 2 uses; another small batch would unlock it as a 'reusable' word.

highlights: First all-past-tense story in the library — でした carries gentle nostalgia.
Hook 「昨日、雨でした。」 anchors time + weather + tense in three tokens.
Bridges yesterday morning, friend's morning, and yesterday night in 8 sentences.
weaknesses: Closer 「いい朝でした」 reuses the proven 'X、いい朝です' pattern from stories 4/5/6/7/10 — fresh tense, familiar shape.
[Closer rewritten 2026-04-22 (v0.14) to break the いい〜です template echo across stories 1-9.]

suggestions.what: First past-tense story sets a precedent. If story 13 returns to present tense, mark the shift consciously — the closer's tense should match the scene's primary frame.

highlights: First N4 grammar (〜と思います) introduced — narrator now interprets, not just observes.
Cat (sleeping on chair, yesterday too) gets multi-time existence — first temporal continuity for a non-human character.
Closer 「静かな月、夜だと思います」 feels like the story's own conclusion, not a tag.
weaknesses: Two と思います uses are structurally similar (both noun-だ-と-思います); future stories should diversify the construction.
suggestions: When 思います gets occ ≥ 3, consider 思う (plain) for variety in nested thoughts.

highlights: The reading-morning setup is immediately legible and pedagogically clean.
〜とき is introduced in a concrete, repeatable context the learner can reuse.
The cat beat gives the quiet room a small emotional lift without adding vocabulary burden.
weaknesses: The scene stays intentionally modest, so originality is lower than the strongest recent entries.
The closing lands gently rather than with a particularly sharp final image.

suggestions.what: If the story is revised later, consider a slightly more vivid final visual detail near the window.
A tiny contrast beat before the ending could make the calmness feel more earned.

highlights: The moon/window opening is immediate and concrete.
The letter motif now survives into the preparation phase, which makes the story feel less list-like.
The final line returns to the moon and gives the piece a cleaner circular close.
weaknesses: The voice remains intentionally restrained, so the story is more elegant than surprising.
The emotional movement is subtle and may feel slight compared with the strongest recent entries.

suggestions.what: In a future revision, consider one more sensory detail from the room to deepen atmosphere.
A slightly more specific relation between the friend and tomorrow morning could sharpen originality further.

highlights: The door-to-sky turn gives the story a small but clear change of focus.
〜てから appears twice in an easy-to-feel action sequence.
The final image of the quiet sky gives the story a gentle close.
weaknesses: The voice is restrained and therefore less characterful than the strongest stories in the set.
Some predicate lines remain very simple, prioritizing clarity over vividness.

suggestions.what: In a future revision, one more sensory detail outside the door could strengthen atmosphere.
A slightly more specific reason for going out could sharpen the story's individuality.

highlights: Breaks the night/window/letter rotation that had dominated stories 11-15 by moving the camera to a kitchen at breakfast — a setting unused since story 3.
First real workout for G017_de_means (で as location-of-action) after 15 stories of dormancy: 机で待ちます, 机で食べます, 二人で食べます.
First scene where the two participants actually act as one (二人で一緒に朝ごはんを食べます) rather than as parallel narrators.
weaknesses: The prose is competent rather than electric; the breakfast vignette is a well-trodden form for graded readers in general.
The s5/s6 parallel (bread warm / tea also warm) verges on filler and could earn its place with a sharper second half.

suggestions.what: Consider dropping 友達 from the s8 closer image — the friend is implied by 一緒に, and trimming it makes the fragment land cleaner
If a future revision allows it, swap 'I wait at the desk' for a brief sensory detail (the chair already pulled out, an empty cup) — would lift voice further

highlights: Introduces the first child in the 17-story library and the first emotional/expressive verb (笑います), so the cast and the verb palette both get their first new register since story 6 (cat).

The arc has a small social pull-forward: the narrator walks past, the child notices and smiles, the narrator smiles back, the narrator stays, then a friend arrives — the bench accumulates company without anyone speaking. That is the kind of beat the closed vocabulary can actually carry.
Closer 'ベンチで、一緒に笑います' echoes the subtitle prop (ベンチ) and the new verb (笑います) without resorting to the overused 〜いい〜です template that 8 of the previous 15 stories defaulted to.
weaknesses: Hook is functional but not sensory: '夕方、私は公園で歩きます' establishes time + place + action cleanly but does not give the reader a noticing moment the way story 1's rain or story 6's cat-in-the-window did. Within the vocabulary I had, I did not find a stronger first line.

The child has no description (no age, no posture, no clothing) because every relevant adjective — small, young, alone — is outside the closed vocabulary. The reader must do the imaginative lifting; I cannot fix this without breaking the vocabulary contract.

suggestions.what: When 小さい / 嬉しい / 元気 enter vocab, revisit s2 to give the child a single physical attribute.
Future story could pair 子供 with 寝ます (the child falling asleep on the bench) to reuse W00038, currently last seen in story 12.
suggestions.why: library reinforcement (Check 6 starvation alarm flagged W00038)

highlights: Five starvation-alarmed words (寝ます, 椅子, 花, 濡れる, 散歩) all land naturally inside one rainy-morning vignette rather than being scattered across paragraphs — the 'yesterday's walk's flowers on the desk' image alone reuses three of them in a single beat.

Brings the cat back from story 6 onto a specific piece of furniture (椅子) instead of just 'somewhere in the room', which is concrete enough to feel like a continuation of the same household instead of a generic motif.

Three structurally distinct new-word introductions: 鳥 anchors the scene (a stranger arrives), 小さい qualifies it twice in adjacent sentences so the descriptor sticks, and 嬉しい lands on the narrator (not on an inanimate object), keeping the inner-state attribution honest.
weaknesses: Closer 'A happy, quiet morning' (嬉しい、静かな朝です) is structurally close to the over-used 〜、いい朝/夜/気分です template flagged in NOTES_FOR_FUTURE_AGENTS.md. It avoids the literal いい but the silhouette is the same Adj + Noun + です shape. I scored closure 3 to reflect this — the image (a happy, quiet morning) is fine, the form is well-trodden.
Hook 'This morning, outside is rain' is competent but conventional — three of the previous 17 stories also open on weather. I scored hook 3.

suggestions.what: When 降ります or a verb of weather-action enters vocab, revise s0 to '今朝、雨が降ります' so the opening is an action rather than a static state.
Future story could pair 鳥 with 笑います (the bird sings, narrator smiles) to extend both the new vocabulary and the new emotional-verb register.

highlights: Brings 思います back into rotation after a 6-story absence (last seen story 13) and uses BOTH constructions in adjacent sentences: literary 'X を思います' (recall someone) at s5, then standard 'X は…だと思います' (think that X is) at s6. That's the right way to teach a verb's range — show both surfaces in context, don't just bump the count.

Two-story arc: the child from story 17's park comes back as an absent presence in the narrator's thought, glued in by 昨日 + 子供 + 思います. The library now has continuity between consecutive stories without recycling settings — a goal NOTES_FOR_FUTURE_AGENTS flagged as the highest-scoring pattern (story 6 / 8 / 10).

Three new words land in three structurally distinct slots: 庭 anchors the scene (s0, s2, s4, s8 — title carrier), 大きい sets up a size juxtaposition with 小さい from story 18 (subtitle pair), 元気 lands twice on different subjects (the bird, then the child) so the descriptor's range is shown.
Closer 'Garden is quiet, sky is big' is a parallel two-clause that callbacks both s0 and s4, structurally different from the over-used 〜いい朝/夜/気分です template the audit warned against.
weaknesses: The two adjacent 思います sentences (s5, s6) are a deliberate teaching choice but read slightly mechanical on the page — a learner sees the verb introduced, then immediately re-used in a different construction. It's pedagogically right, narratively a touch laboured. I considered breaking them up with a present-tense observation but decided the consolidation was worth the small bump in formality.

S7 'I smile.' is a short single-content-token sentence; I shortened it from 'I also smile too' (which uses も) because the closer already echoes the parallel structure and a quieter beat felt right. Some reviewers might score this lower as a hook-loss; I left it because the voice is the point of the beat.

suggestions.what: If 静かな (na-attributive) gets explicit drilling later, revisit s8 to write '静かな庭、大きい空' as a fully attributive parallel.
Future story could pair 元気 with a verb subject (child being energetic in a scene) rather than predicative 元気です, to round out the na-adj grammar.

reviews.title_en: A Letter on a Cold Morning
highlights: Brings 手紙 (W00039) back from the starvation list (last seen story 14) and uses it five times across five different syntactic positions: object of 書きます (s1, s2, s3), topic of あります (s6), title carrier (title, s8). That is the right way to retire a starvation alarm — not just hit the count, but exercise the word's range.

First time the library has a parallel cold/warm contrast inside a single story: 寒い lands on 外 (s0, s5) and 朝 (s8); 温かい lands on 手紙 (s2), お茶 (s5), 手紙 again (s8). The closer 'cold morning, warm letter' uses the new descriptor pair structurally — emotional warmth shipped as physical warmth.

Continuity beat: the friend (story 16, 17) is again offstage, and the letter (story 10/11/14) is the bridge — but the framing is fresh because we're at the writer's end of the letter, not the reader's. Different camera angle on a familiar prop.
weaknesses: S4 'I write my name too' is structurally the lightest sentence in the story (3 content tokens, no descriptor) — it earns its place pedagogically (pushes 名前 to occ=2) but reads slightly utilitarian on the page. I considered cutting it but the alternative was to let 名前 ship with occ=1, which is the exact pattern that triggered the recent starvation cascade.

The 'tomorrow the letter will be by my friend' construction (s6) uses あります for a future state — technically correct but slightly stiff in English; the gloss leans on 'will be' to convey the implicit future. I left it because 明日 + あります is a legitimate N5 pattern.

suggestions.what: When 出します (mail/send) enters vocab, revise s6 to '明日、手紙を出します' for a more active future.
Future story could pair 寒い with 雪 (snow) for the next seasonal beat.

highlights: Clears three story-15 starvation entries in one scene: 帰ります (s1), 夜 (s0, s8), 月 (s4, s5, s7) — none had been seen since story 15. The walk is genuinely about going home alone, not a contrived ride for the words.
First non-rain weather noun (雪) and first walking-surface noun (道) — both arrive in the same image (snow on the road) so the pair is taught together, not as separate flashcards.
Closer 'A solitary night is quiet.' uses the new 一人 attributively (一人の夜) instead of predicatively (私は一人です) — small grammatical exercise that lifts the closer above the 〜いい〜です silhouette.
weaknesses: Six of the nine sentences are X は Y です predicates — the rhythm reads slightly catalogue-like in the middle (s3-s6). I could break it up by using a single て-form chain, but every form-mixing option I tried (e.g. 'snow falls and the road is quiet') needs a verb the library doesn't have yet (降る). Decision: ship the catalogue and let story 22+ vary the predicate shapes.
The 'big moon, small snow' visual juxtaposition in s5/s6 is the structural payoff but reads slightly diagrammatic — the pedagogical point (size pair) is doing more work than the image.

suggestions.what: When 降ります (to fall) enters vocab, revisit s0 to '雪が降ります' for an active opening.
Pair 一人 with 二人 in a future story for explicit count contrast.

reviews.title_en: A Photo in the Old Book
highlights: Brings 二人 back from story 16 (3-occ since) and pairs it with a memory-bearing concrete prop (写真) that the library has never had before — the photo is the bridge between past (s2/s3) and present (s4/s6) without the story needing past tense.

New descriptor pair (新しい / 古い) is taught contrastively in the same scene rather than scattered: 古い anchors s0/s2/s7 (the book / the photo), 新しい answers in s5/s6/s7 (the new book on the same desk). The closer s7 'old photo, new book' is the structural payoff.

S4 'My friend looks at the photo and smiles' — first time in 22 stories that a non-narrator character does an observable emotional action (笑います) directly, not via a と思います inference. That's a small but important voice expansion.
weaknesses: Three あります sentences (s0, s2, s5) are stylistically homogeneous — the descriptor sits before the noun in each, the predicate is identical. Reads slightly catalogue-like; I considered swapping one for an active verb but every alternative needed a verb the library doesn't have (置く / 置いてある / 写る). Decision: ship the three あります and let story 23+ vary the existence-predicate shapes.

Closer 'the morning of the two of us is warm' attributes 温かい to a morning, which is well-attested literary usage but borderline relative to the audit's 'inanimate-thing-is-X' rule. Justified here because the previous sentences have built the warmth concretely (two people, shared book, smile).

suggestions.what: When 撮ります (to photograph) enters vocab, future story could pair 写真 with a verb of taking, not just existing.
Pair 古い with 椅子 in a future story — old chair, new sit.

highlights: First public-transit scene in 23 stories — 駅 + 電車 give the library a setting it has never had: infrastructure, the city's quiet edge. The scene fits the existing voice (slow, observed, single 'I') without leaning on the new vocabulary as a gimmick.

Direct emotional pair to story 21: same outdoor evening, same snowy road, but the human-count noun has flipped from 一人 (alone) to 二人 (two) — the narrator who walked home alone in 21 is now the narrator who waits and walks home with someone in 23. That is the cross-story arc NOTES_FOR_FUTURE_AGENTS flagged as the highest-scoring pattern.

Closer 'Together, an evening of stars' uses 一緒に attributively-by-implication and ends on a concrete image (星) the s7 already established, so it satisfies the lonely-noun lint *and* the variety guard against 〜いい〜です.
weaknesses: Three 来ます verbs in a row (s3 雪が, s4 電車が, s5 友達は) — the repetition is intentional (each 'arrival' pulls the scene forward) but reads as four beats with the same verb if the reader is paying attention to the JP. Could vary by using 着きます (to arrive) but that verb isn't in the library yet.
S2 'The station's clock is big' is the lightest beat — it earns its place pedagogically (introduces 時計, sets up s3) but is not narratively interesting on its own.

suggestions.what: When 着きます (to arrive) enters vocab, revisit s4/s5 to differentiate train-arrives vs friend-arrives.
Future story could pair 駅 with 朝 (morning station, departure beat) for symmetry with this story's evening arrival.

highlights: Clears five starvation entries in one cohesive scene: 朝ごはん / 食べます / 卵 / ドア (story 16 cluster) and reuses 花 / 庭 — none feels shoehorned because the scene is genuinely about a spring breakfast outdoors.

First seasonal vocabulary in the library — 春 lands three times in three different positions (subject of 来ます at s0, attributive 春の朝 at s2, attributive 春の道 at s8). The seasonal turn is shipped as content, not just announced.
開けます is taught on two different objects in adjacent sentences (s1 窓, s4 ドア) — the verb's range is shown in real grammar, not via flashcards.

Closer 'long spring road, warm morning' uses the new 長い + 春 plus existing 温かい + 朝 — every adjective is doing semantic work (the long road implies a journey ahead, the warm morning is the spring beat). Not a generic Adj+Noun list.
weaknesses: S7 'The road is long.' is a single-content predicate beat that arrives a bit abruptly after the breakfast tableau — the road has no prior anchor in the scene. Considered cutting it but 長い needs two occurrences and this is the only natural slot.

Six predicate-です sentences across the story — that is the high-frequency shape the library leans on. Could vary more if the library had more verbs, but the verbs we have (来る / 開ける / ある / 食べる) are already used.

suggestions.what: When 出ます (to go out) enters vocab, revisit s4 to '庭に出ます' for an active going-out beat.
Future story could pair 春 with 鳥 (returning birds) for a layered seasonal scene.

highlights: First sad story in 25 stories — the library has been mostly cozy contentment, and NOTES_FOR_FUTURE_AGENTS explicitly hints that brave thematic choices score higher on originality than another competent quiet-morning. The narrator is briefly left behind, sees an old photo, registers the loss, then steps out into the rain. No resolution, no consolation — the closer leaves the weight on the reader.

Voice is the strongest in the library so far: every sentence is one short observation, no commentary, no explanation. The 'I am sad' beat at s5 lands because the four sentences before it have built the room, the leaving, the photo, the smile — the reader feels the sadness before they read the word.

Cross-story memory: the friend in the photo is smiling — that is a callback to the same friend's smile in story 17 (park) and story 22 (book reading), but here read in absence rather than presence. The library's accumulated continuity does emotional work for the first time.
weaknesses: Closer 'A sad morning, a quiet rain' uses the Adj-Noun, Adj-na-Noun shape that the variety guard warns about. I scored closure 4 (not 5) honestly — the form is a known silhouette even though the content is fresh.
Six predicate-です sentences again — the library's persistent rhythmic limit. With more verbs the prose could vary more; with the verbs we have I cannot. This is structural, not authorial.

suggestions.what: When 泣きます (to cry) enters vocab, future story could continue this emotional register.
Future story could pair 部屋 with 椅子 to give the indoor private space more furniture.

highlights: Cross-story callback the reader earns: in story 20 the narrator wrote a letter to a friend on a cold morning; in story 26 the friend's reply is waiting on the desk. The library is now doing what only libraries can do — paying off setup beats from earlier stories. No commentary needed, the letter just appears.

Spatial-verb pair completion: 入ります enters with two adjacent uses (s4 home, s5 room) so its range is taught immediately, and it pairs structurally with 出ます from story 25 (which left a room). Stories 25 and 26 thus form an emotional and grammatical contrast: leave a sad empty room → enter a warm welcoming home.

First story to use 鍵 as a story-anchoring object — the small concrete prop the scene turns on. The library has had photos (22) and letters (10/11/14/20) as anchors, but never a tool that the protagonist actively uses.
weaknesses: 11 sentences with 6 ending in です is the same rhythmic limit the library has always had — until verbs like 立ちます/座ります/聞きます enter vocab, this won't change.

Closer 'small key, warm home' is a list-pair shape similar to story 20's 'cold morning, warm letter'. The motif is fresh (key vs morning) but the silhouette is recognisable. Closure 4 honestly reflects this.

suggestions.what: Future story could use the key to unlock something not a door (a box, a desk drawer) for object variety.

highlights: Brings 思います back into rotation a second time (last seen story 20) and shows it in the most useful pattern for graded readers — i-adj + と思います ('I think the garden is beautiful'). Story 19 had taught the noun + だと thread; story 27 fills in the i-adjective thread, completing the と思います surface coverage learners actually need.

Same-place / different-season pairing: the garden from story 19 (autumn-mood quiet morning, child memory) returns in summer (cat sleeping, bird visiting, tea outside, beautiful flowers). One concrete location, two temperatures, two emotional registers — this is the structural variety NOTES_FOR_FUTURE_AGENTS asks for ('add a new character, prop, or setting' rather than recycle one variable).

美しい finally enters the library — the first aesthetic adjective. Until story 27 the only positive descriptors were 温かい (warm), いい (good), 嬉しい (happy). 'Beautiful' opens a register the prose did not have.
weaknesses: Closer 'A hot summer, a beautiful garden' uses the Adj + Noun, Adj + Noun list shape that is by now a recognisable closer silhouette in the library. Closure 4 honestly reflects this; the new-vocabulary content (暑い, 美しい) does the heavy lifting that prevents a 3.
11 sentences and again 6 of them end in です. Until verbs like 立ちます/座ります/書きます (other than 書く introduced in 20) enter further, the rhythm cannot diversify.

suggestions.what: When 鳴きます (to chirp/make a sound) enters vocab, garden scenes can include the bird actually singing rather than just 'being near the flowers'.
Story 28 should introduce a new outdoor verb (e.g. 座ります) so future garden/park scenes can show the protagonist actually doing things instead of being.

highlights: Two long-overdue verbs land in one story: 座ります (sit) and 買います (buy). Until story 28 the library had 椅子 in vocab since story 12 but no verb for sitting on it — characters could only 'be on' chairs by 寝ます (sleep). 28 fixes this and immediately demonstrates 座ります twice (s4, s8) on the same prop with a different actor — the right way to teach a verb's range.

First commerce verb 買います opens an entire register the library has not had: scenes where the protagonist actually exchanges something with the world rather than just observing it. Pairs with 飲みます at s10 to show the buy-then-consume sequence.

Cross-story callback to story 23's station and the same friend the narrator has been corresponding with since story 20 (write letter) → story 26 (read letter at home). Three-story emotional arc paying off.
weaknesses: 12 sentences with 7 ending in です/verb-polite — the cadence is still uniform. Once 立ちます enters and te-form chains can replace the every-sentence-is-a-clause shape, the prose can breathe.

Closer 'A rainy morning, a warm shop' is the same Adj+Noun, Adj+Noun shape used in stories 20 (cold morning, warm letter) and 26 (small key, warm home). The library has a closer rut — closure 4 honestly reflects it. The fix is grammatical (need で-form sentence connectors) not just thematic.

suggestions.what: Story 29 should attempt at least one closer NOT in the Adj+Noun, Adj+Noun shape.
When 立ちます (stand) lands, the 座ります/立ちます pair will let scenes show transitions explicitly.

next_actions: All 15 stories now pass the engagement bar (avg ≥ 3.5; every dimension ≥ 3).
Underused N5 grammar still worth load-bearing use: G004_ni_location (still atmospheric only) and G006_kara_from (only 2 uses, both in story 10).
First N4 grammar (G014_to_omoimasu) introduced in story 12. Next-best N4 candidates per the catalog: 〜たい (want), 〜ことができる (can do), 〜たら (when/if).
Stories 4 / 5 / 6 / 7 / 8 / 11 / 12 all rate 4.4+; revisit candidates if needed: story 1 (3.8) and story 3 (4.0) — but neither is below the 3.5 bar.
Closure motif 'X、いい朝です' has been reused 5 times. Future stories should diversify the closer pattern.
Authoring guidance now embedded in pipeline/authoring_rules.md Section 6 (engagement & voice) — ships with writer prompt.
